get.description: Return a nonce for the user with wallet=address, if any.

post.description: Verify signature, return JWT with sub=iam.uid

Generate a new invite key for a user IAM.
The authenticated IAM must own the user_uid IAM.
Enforces MAX_INVITES_PER_IAM limit.

get.description:
List all invite keys created by a user IAM.
The authenticated IAM must own the user_uid IAM.

delete.description:
Delete specific unused invite keys by IDs.
The authenticated IAM must own the user_uid IAM.
Only deletes unused invites.

get.description:
Check if registration is allowed with this invite key.
Returns whether the key exists, is unused, and not expired.
Public endpoint - no authentication required.

post.description:
Register a new IAM account with wallet signature and invite key.

Creates a fully independent IAM using atomic JanusGraph operation.

Flow:
1. Validate invite key (exists, unused, not expired)
2. Verify message signature matches wallet address
3. Check username (email) is unique
4. Get creator IAM (who generated the invite key)
5. Atomically create IAM with all relationships and guild in JanusGraph
6. Mark invite key as used
7. Check admin status (should be True)
8. Generate JWT token
9. Return token and IAM info

Final relationship structure (fully independent):
- IAM --OWNS--> IAM (self-owned)
- creator --CREATED--> IAM (creator relationship)
- creator --OWNED--> IAM (historical ownership)
- NO ADMINISTERS edges (fully independent admin)
- IAM has own Guild (username as guild name)

Login with email or username and password.

Returns JWT token with sub=iam.uid

Register a new IAM account with email/password.

Creates a fully independent IAM with email authentication.
If invite_key is not provided, HEAD is used as the creator.

Flow:
1. Validate invite key (if provided) or get HEAD as creator
2. Validate password strength
3. Check email uniqueness
4. Check username uniqueness
5. Hash password
6. Create IAM with email and password_hash
7. Mark invite key as used (if provided)
8. Generate JWT token

Exchange a valid (non-expired) JWT for a fresh one with a new expiry.

The client should call this proactively before the token expires
(e.g. when less than 1 hour remains) or reactively on any 401 response.
If the token is already expired this returns 403 — the user must log in again.

get.description: Return the authenticated user's profile (username, email, name).

patch.description: Update the authenticated user's username and/or email. Requires current password.

post.description:
Link a wallet address to an existing account.

Allows email/password users to add Web3 authentication.

get.description:
Return all account state needed for frontend routing decisions in a single call.

Frontend routing rules:
- Not authenticated                     → /login
- has_payment_method == false           → /start/plan  (plan + card setup required)
- has_first_face == false               → /start/create/name  (create first face)
- onboarding_complete == true           → proceed to main app

Called immediately after login/signup and after returning from Stripe Checkout.

post.description:
Create a new API key for programmatic access.

This endpoint requires JWT authentication (web app).
The generated API key can then be used for V1 API access.

Optional access controls:
- budget_usd: spend cap in USD (null = unlimited)
- allowed_face_ids: list of face USERNAMES this key may access (null = all owned faces)
- allowed_llm_models: list of LLM model names this key may use (null = all)

**IMPORTANT**: The full API key is only shown once. Store it securely!

get.description:
List all API keys for the authenticated user.

Returns all keys (including revoked) with metadata but NOT the actual key values.
Includes budget/spend tracking and allowlist configuration.
allowed_face_ids is returned as face usernames (stored as UUIDs internally).

schema.title: Response List Api Keys V1 Auth Api Keys Get

delete.description:
Revoke an API key.

Revoked keys cannot be used for authentication but remain in the database for audit purposes.

patch.description:
Update an API key's metadata, budget, and access control allowlists.

allowed_face_ids accepts face USERNAMES (not UUIDs).

Fields:
- name: rename the key
- budget_usd: set a new spend cap (null = no change; use clear_budget=true to remove)
- reset_spent: set spent_usd back to 0
- allowed_face_ids: replace face allowlist using face usernames (null = no change; use clear_iam_allowlist=true to remove)
- allowed_llm_models: replace LLM allowlist (null = no change; use clear_llm_allowlist=true to remove)
- clear_budget: remove spend cap (set budget_usd to null)
- clear_iam_allowlist: remove face restriction (key accesses all owned faces)
- clear_llm_allowlist: remove LLM restriction (key may use all LLM models)

Requires JWT authentication (not API key auth).

post.description:
Create a new face.

This wraps IAM creation with a face-oriented interface.

get.description: List all faces owned by the authenticated user.

Return compile quota + chat usage stats for all owned faces in one call.

Response is a dict keyed by face username. Each entry contains:
- Compile quota (messages and tokens) with plan limits
- Last sync timestamp
- Aggregate chat billing usage (input/output tokens and total cost)

schema.title: Response Get Faces Stats V1 Faces Stats Get

get.description:
Compare geometric centroids of multiple owned faces.

Returns a symmetric pairwise cosine similarity matrix for all 4 components
(face, beta, delta, epsilon). Raw vectors are never exposed.

All requested faces must be owned by the authenticated user.
Requires at least 2 faces.

parameters.name: faces

schema.description: Comma-separated list of owned face usernames (min 2)
schema.title: Faces

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of owned face usernames (min 2)

get.description: Get a specific face by ID (username).

delete.description:
Delete a face and all its associated data.

Drops the IAM vertex from JanusGraph (auto-removing all its edges) and
deletes its Postgres rooms/documents and Qdrant face centroid. Belief,
type, and entity vertices are intentionally left as orphans — they can be
rediscovered via similarity search and reused by future faces.

get.description:
Find the k nearest (or furthest) owned faces by centroid similarity.

Scoped to faces owned by the authenticated user.
The queried face must also be owned by the user.
Raw vectors are never exposed — only cosine similarity scores.

post.description:
Transcribe an audio or video file using AssemblyAI with speaker diarization.
Returns raw transcript text and speaker-labelled segments.
Charged to the user's credit account (USD). Does NOT create any document or thread.
The client should use the returned text to call POST /faces/{face_id}/upload.

Create a compile document or thread from an uploaded file.

- type=document (default): flat text blob stored as a compile document.
  perspective=first-person means the face wrote it; third-person means it's about the face.
  Accepts text, PDF, or audio/video (monologue transcription).

- type=thread: parsed as a multi-speaker 'Speaker: message' transcript.
  face_speaker maps one speaker name to role='user' (the face);
  all others become role='assistant' (the interviewer/coach).
  Audio/video + type=thread returns 400 — use POST /transcribe first.

schema.title: Response Upload Face Source File V1 Faces  Face Id  Upload Post

post.description:
Import a YouTube video as a compile document or thread.

Downloads the video with yt-dlp, extracts audio with ffmpeg, then
transcribes via AssemblyAI — identical billing and response shape
as POST /faces/{face_id}/upload with an audio file.

schema.title: Response Import Face Source Url V1 Faces  Face Id  Import Post

post.description:
Create a chat completion.

OpenAI-compatible transparent proxy. Model field accepts:
  - "face@model-name"  — explicit model override
  - "face"             — uses face's default_model
Start a new compile thread for conversational training.

get.description: List all compile threads across all owned model IAMs.

schema.title: Response List Compile Threads V1 Compile Threads Get

post.description: Send a user message to a compile thread and receive an assistant reply.

patch.description: Replace all messages in a compile thread (e.g. after upload, before sync).

post.description:
Sync (archive) a compile thread to the knowledge graph.

Uploaded/imported threads (compile_upload, compile_import) have their token
quota charged here at sync time — matching the document sync behaviour.
Live interview threads (compile_v1, compile_interview) are charged per user
message in send_compile_message, so no additional charge is applied here.

get.description: Get a compile thread with full message history and quota state.

delete.description: Delete a compile thread and remove its contributions from the knowledge graph.

post.description: Submit a new document for a model. Token count is a preview — charged at sync.

get.description: List all compile documents across all owned model IAMs.

schema.title: Response List Compile Documents V1 Compile Documents Get

patch.description: Edit a compile document (label, content, or perspective). Auto-versions on content change.

get.description: Get a single compile document by ID.

delete.description: Delete a compile document and remove its contributions from the knowledge graph.

post.description: Sync (archive) a compile document to the knowledge graph. Charges token quota.

Run LLM extraction on a document, populating running_extraction for the next sync.

This step MUST be called before sync whenever the document content changes.
It chunks the content into ~250-word pieces and runs DSPy extraction on each,
building up facts, beliefs, types, and entities in running_extraction.

After prepare completes, call POST /compile/documents/{id}/sync to archive
the extracted knowledge to the graph and charge token quota.

get.description:
Return every supported LLM with its base pricing and the authenticated
user's all-time usage on that model in a single response.

Rows are ordered by user_total_cost_usd descending (most-used first),
with zero-spend models appended alphabetically at the end.

This lets the frontend render a pricing table where each row shows
the per-token rates alongside how much the user has actually spent.

schema.title: Response Get Llm Costs V1 Billing Llm Costs Get

get.description: Return the credit balance and frozen status for the authenticated user.

Return aggregated usage data grouped by a chosen dimension.

Supported group_by values:
- api_key_id: group by which API key made the request
- model_iam_id: group by which model IAM was accessed
- llm_model: group by the underlying LLM used
- model_username: group by model IAM username (recorded at request time)
- date: group by calendar date (UTC)

All filters and groups apply only to rows owned by the authenticated user.

parameters.name: group_by

schema.description: Grouping dimension: api_key_id | model_iam_id | llm_model | model_username | date
schema.title: Group By

parameters.description: Grouping dimension: api_key_id | model_iam_id | llm_model | model_username | date

parameters.name: llm_model

schema.description: Filter by underlying LLM model name
schema.title: Llm Model

parameters.description: Filter by underlying LLM model name

post.description:
Top up credit balance.

Two paths:
- **Stripe path (default):** Omit `payment_ref`. Requires a saved card on file
  (set via POST /billing/card-setup). Creates and confirms a Stripe PaymentIntent
  server-side, then credits the account. Minimum $1.00.
- **Admin/test path:** Supply a unique `payment_ref` to bypass Stripe and record
  a credit directly (idempotent — duplicate refs are silently ignored).

Create a Stripe Checkout session in 'setup' mode to collect and save a
payment method without charging the user.

Returns a `checkout_url` — redirect the user there. After they enter their
card, Stripe redirects back to STRIPE_SUCCESS_URL and fires a
`setup_intent.succeeded` webhook which stores the payment method ID in the
user's account (enabling POST /billing/topup without payment_ref).

get.description:
Return the authenticated user's current subscription plan, status,
IAM count vs plan limit, and renewal date.

Return user-level compile token quota and per-face build stats.

tokens_used / tokens_limit / tokens_remaining reflect the single shared
bucket for the authenticated user across all their faces.

Per-face stats include:
- tokens_in_graph: tokens currently compiled in the knowledge graph (decrements on delete)
- tokens_charged_period: tokens charged to quota this billing period (resets with user quota)
- tokens_charged_alltime: cumulative tokens charged since account creation (never resets)

post.description:
Create a Stripe Checkout session for upgrading to standard or pro plan.
Returns a checkout_url the client should redirect the user to.

parameters.name: plan

schema.description: Subscription plan to upgrade to: 'standard' or 'pro'
schema.title: Plan

parameters.description: Subscription plan to upgrade to: 'standard' or 'pro'

get.description:
Return the authenticated user's Stripe invoice history.

Each item includes the date, amount, status, and a direct link to the
Stripe-hosted PDF — no file storage required on our end.
Returns an empty list if the user has no Stripe customer record yet.

schema.title: Response List Invoices V1 Billing Invoices Get

post.description:
Stripe webhook receiver. Verified via Stripe-Signature header — no user auth.

Handled events:
- checkout.session.completed         → primary subscription provisioning
- customer.subscription.created      → secondary provisioning (dashboard/manual subs)
- customer.subscription.updated      → plan upgrades, downgrades, renewals
- customer.subscription.deleted      → downgrade to free
- invoice.payment_failed             → mark past_due (grace period, no immediate downgrade)
- invoice.payment_action_required    → mark past_due
- setup_intent.succeeded             → save payment method from card-setup flow
- payment_intent.succeeded           → idempotent credit topup (async fallback)
- payment_intent.payment_failed      → log payment failure

patch.description:
Enable or disable credit-account billing when compile token quota is exhausted.

When enabled, syncs that exceed the plan quota are charged to the credit account
at the training model's per-token rate (+ markup) instead of returning 429.
Requires a positive credit balance to enable.

post.description:
Cancel the authenticated user's subscription at period end.
The user retains paid plan access until current_period_end.

Anthropic-native transparent proxy.

Drop-in replacement for https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages.
Point your Anthropic client at DeepSelf's base URL, swap the API key,
and set model to "iam_username@claude-sonnet-4-6".

DeepSelf will:
  1. Authenticate the caller
  2. Build the persona profile for iam_username
  3. Inject it into the system field
  4. Forward the full request to Anthropic with our system API key
  5. Stream / return the response verbatim
  6. Deduct token usage from the caller's credit balance

OpenAI Responses API transparent proxy.

Drop-in replacement for https://api.openai.com/v1/responses.
Point your OpenAI client at DeepSelf's base URL, swap the API key,
and set model to "iam_username@gpt-4o".

DeepSelf will:
  1. Authenticate the caller
  2. Build the persona profile for iam_username
  3. Inject it into the instructions field
  4. Forward the full request to OpenAI with our system API key
  5. Stream / return the response verbatim
  6. Deduct token usage from the caller's credit balance

get.description: Return the full knowledge graph for a face. Admin only.
